mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving immediate positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses below the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient in a rolling 3-year regression remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or exhibits negative predictive power for forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a high-inflation environment signals supply chain bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and trigger earnings downgrades, resulting in negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where industrial production falls while CPI exceeds 3.0 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in real consumer credit outstanding indicates excessive household leverage that precedes a consumption pullback, causing immediate negative equity returns as earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive rank correlation over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in the federal funds rate raises the cost of capital and reduces the present value of distant cash flows, systematically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower during rate-hiking periods compared to stable-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion that supports aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in real GDP growth above 2.5 percent signals strong underlying economic momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing immediate positive equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where quarterly GDP growth crosses above the 2.5 percent threshold.
